source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) are encrypted

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) are encrypted
input_row.description: Amazon Machine Images should utilize EBS Encrypted snapshots
input_row.rationale: AMIs backed by EBS snapshots should use EBS encryption. Snapshot volumes can be encrypted and attached to an AMI.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine AMIs are encrypted: From the Console: 1. Login to the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. In the left pane click Instances, click AMIs. 3. In the Details tab. 4. Review the 'Block Devices' 5. Confirm that it ends with encrypted. If it doesn't end with encrypted, refer to the remediation below. From the Command Line: 1. Run the aws ec2 describe-images command aws ec2 describe-images --region us-east-1 --owner self --filter "Name=block- device-mapping.encrypted,Values=false" --query "Images[*].[ImageId]" 2. If this produces a list of AMI's make note as these are not encrypted, then refer to the remediation below.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to encrypt AMI EBS Snapshots: From the Console: 1. Login to the EC2 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ec2/. 2. In the left pane click on AMIs. 3. Select the AMI that does not comply to the encryption policy. 4. Click on Actions. 5. Click on Copy AMI. Destination region - `Select the region the AMI is in`. Name - `Enter the new Name` Description - `Enter the new description` Encryption - `Select` Encrypt target EBS snapshots 6. Click on Copy AMI Once the AMI has finished copying. 7. Select the AMI that does not have encrypted EBS snapshots. 8. Click on Actions. 9. Click on Deregister From the Command Line: 1. Run the aws ec2 copy-image command to copy AMI with encrypted block device aws ec2 copy-image --name <New_AMI_Name> --source-image-id <Image-ID> -- source-region <region> --encrypted 2. Run aws ec2 deregister-image to deregister older AMIs aws ec2 deregister-image --image-id <Image-ID> References: 1. https://aws.amazon.com/premiumsupport/knowledge-center/view-ami-snapshot- encryption-details/ 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/WindowsGuide/AMIEncryption.ht ml 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/ec2/copy-image.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/ec2/deregister-image.html 5. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_DeregisterIma ge.html 6. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_CopyImage.ht ml

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be fully verified through AWS APIs/boto3/AWS CLI. The audit process provided includes a command-line method using AWS CLI to determine if AMIs are encrypted and this indicates that the necessary information is accessible via API calls. The requirement to check AMI encryption status involves querying properties of EC2 instances that are typically exposed through AWS services via the CLI and boto3 SDK.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ec2_ami_ebs_encryption_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API Call:** Use the `boto3` library to interact with AWS EC2 service.
- **Method:** Call `boto3.client('ec2').describe_images()` to retrieve details of AMIs owned by the user.
- **Filter Criteria:** Filter the returned images to check the 'BlockDeviceMappings' section for each AMI to see if the block devices have encryption enabled.
- **CLI Equivalent:** Use the AWS CLI command `aws ec2 describe-images --region <region> --owner self --query 'Images[*].BlockDeviceMappings[*].Ebs.Encrypted'`.
- **Specific Properties:** Verify that each AMI's block device mapping includes 'Ebs' with 'Encrypted' set to `True`.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** Iterate over all user-owned AMIs in all relevant AWS regions.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** All block devices associated with an AMI have encryption enabled.
  - **Fail:** If any block device associated with an AMI has encryption disabled.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
